verdict: Accept with minor revisions. The paper presents a novel and technically sound approach combining Trotter and LCU methods for Hamiltonian simulation with significant practical improvements. The theoretical contributions are solid and the numerical demonstrations are convincing. However, the manuscript appears incomplete (truncated text) and needs final polish.
strengths: Novel algorithmic framework that synergistically combines Trotter and LCU methods, inheriting advantages of both
Rigorous mathematical analysis with clear proofs of improved time scaling (1+1/(2K+1) vs 1+1/K) and accuracy dependence
Practical random-sampling implementation requiring only one ancilla qubit with efficient classical sampling procedures
Comprehensive numerical comparisons demonstrating 2-4 orders of magnitude improvement over state-of-the-art Trotter methods
NCC algorithm maintains near-optimal system-size scaling for lattice Hamiltonians while improving accuracy
Clear presentation with helpful figures illustrating the key algorithmic ideas
weaknesses: The manuscript text is truncated/incomplete - the proof of Theorem 1 cuts off mid-sentence
The µ⁴ sampling overhead factor compared to standard algorithms is not sufficiently discussed in terms of practical impact
Higher-order NCC algorithms lack detailed gate count analysis (acknowledged by authors but limits completeness)
Dense notation in some sections may hinder accessibility to broader quantum computing audience
Limited discussion of experimental feasibility and noise robustness for near-term implementations
required_actions: Complete the truncated text - ensure the full manuscript including the complete proof of Theorem 1 is provided
Add more detailed discussion of the practical tradeoffs involving the µ⁴ sampling overhead
Proofread the manuscript for consistency (e.g., 'Department of Computer Science' appears twice in affiliations)
